questions.question: The HuggingFace `pipeline` API wraps:
options: Any model into a simple task-specific interface -- tokenise, infer, decode all in one call
questions.explanation: pipeline('task', model='name') handles all preprocessing/postprocessing. Fastest way to use any HuggingFace model.

questions.question: The `AutoModel` and `AutoTokenizer` classes provide:
options: Architecture-agnostic loading -- same code works for BERT, GPT-2, T5, etc.
questions.explanation: AutoModel.from_pretrained('any-model-name') detects architecture and loads correct class. Eliminates architecture-specific imports.

questions.question: HuggingFace Spaces hosts:
options: Live ML demos and apps built with Gradio or Streamlit -- free, public, shareable
questions.explanation: Spaces = free compute for demos. CPU (always free), GPU (limited free tier). Push a Gradio app and get a public URL instantly.

questions.question: The `datasets` library `map` function is used for:
options: Applying a transformation function to each example -- tokenisation, feature extraction
questions.explanation: dataset.map(tokenize_fn, batched=True) applies tokenisation efficiently to entire dataset with multiprocessing.

questions.question: Model cards on HuggingFace Hub document:
options: Intended use, limitations, training data, evaluation results, bias considerations
questions.explanation: Model cards (Mitchell et al. 2019) provide transparency: what it was trained for, what it should NOT be used for, performance across demographic groups.

questions.question: GGUF format files are used for:
options: Running quantised LLMs locally via llama.cpp without a GPU
questions.explanation: GGUF: successor to GGML. Stores quantised weights (2-8 bit). llama.cpp can run 7B models on CPU-only MacBook at reasonable speed.

questions.question: HuggingFace Inference Endpoints differ from Spaces by:
options: Providing dedicated, scalable, production-grade API endpoints for your models
questions.explanation: Endpoints = managed deployment: auto-scaling, custom hardware, private URLs. Spaces = demos. Endpoints = production APIs.

questions.question: The `push_to_hub` method lets you:
options: Upload models, datasets, tokenisers, or adapters to your HuggingFace account
questions.explanation: trainer.push_to_hub('username/model-name') uploads fine-tuned weights. Share with community or keep private. Version controlled.

questions.question: Transformers library supports which frameworks?
options: PyTorch, TensorFlow, and JAX -- same model, different backends
questions.explanation: AutoModel.from_pretrained() returns PyTorch by default. Pass from_tf=True for TF. Enables framework-agnostic model sharing.

questions.question: The MTEB leaderboard ranks models on:
options: Embedding quality across 58 tasks (retrieval, classification, clustering, STS, etc.)
questions.explanation: Massive Text Embedding Benchmark -- the standard reference for choosing sentence embedding models. Filter by task type for your use case.
